title: China's economy remains on track
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
In 2002, an electronics shop on a dusty street in Hangzhou stood as a quiet witness to China's accelerating transformation. A few years earlier, most customers arrived by bicycle, looking for cassette players or basic radios. Sales were steady, predictable - rooted in a rhythm that hadn't changed much in decades.
